title: LanguageMap
description: A map of BCP 47 language tags to localized text values. Used throughout Herbapedia for multilingual content like names, descriptions, and usage instructions.

propertyNames.description: BCP 47 language tag: ISO 639 language code optionally followed by ISO 3166 region subtag

examples.en: This herb is used to treat nausea and improve digestion.
examples.zh-Hant: 此草藥用於治療噁心和改善消化。
examples.zh-Hans: 此草药用于治疗恶心和改善消化。

supportedLanguages.name: English
supportedLanguages.description: Primary language for all content. Every language map must include English.

supportedLanguages.name: Traditional Chinese (繁體中文)
supportedLanguages.description: Traditional Chinese characters. Used for Taiwan, Hong Kong, and classical texts.

supportedLanguages.name: Simplified Chinese (简体中文)
supportedLanguages.description: Simplified Chinese characters. Used for Mainland China and Singapore.

supportedLanguages.name: Japanese (日本語)
supportedLanguages.description: Japanese. Often relevant for Kampo medicine.

supportedLanguages.name: Korean (한국어)
supportedLanguages.description: Korean. Often relevant for Korean traditional medicine.

supportedLanguages.name: Hindi (हिन्दी)
supportedLanguages.description: Hindi. Used for Ayurvedic content in India.

supportedLanguages.name: Sanskrit (संस्कृतम्)
supportedLanguages.description: Sanskrit. Used for classical Ayurvedic terminology.

supportedLanguages.name: Arabic (العربية)
supportedLanguages.description: Arabic. May be relevant for Unani medicine.

usageNotes: Always provide 'en' as the baseline language
For Chinese content, provide both zh-Hant and zh-Hans when possible
Language tags should match the script actually used (don't use 'zh' alone)
For classical texts, preserve the original language in addition to translations
